public_functions.behavior_description: Initialize GTM-based 3-phase complementary PWM using the unified IfxGtm_Pwm high-level driver. Algorithm: (1) Declare all local configuration objects: IfxGtm_Pwm_Config mainConfig; arrays for IfxGtm_Pwm_OutputConfig (6 outputs for three HS/LS pairs), IfxGtm_Pwm_DtmConfig (3 entries for deadtime), IfxGtm_Pwm_ChannelConfig (3 logical channels: 0..2), and one IfxGtm_Pwm_InterruptConfig. (2) Initialize defaults via IfxGtm_Pwm_initConfig(&mainConfig, &MODULE_GTM). (3) Fill output[] entries to define three complementary HS/LS outputs, set push-pull mode and suitable pad driver, and set both HS and LS active state high. (4) Prepare dtmConfig[] with rising and falling deadtime of 1 microsecond for each complementary pair. (5) Prepare interruptConfig with mode=IfxGtm_IrqMode_pulseNotify, isrProvider=IfxSrc_Tos_cpu0, priority=20, and periodEvent=function pointer to IfxGtm_periodEventFunction; leave dutyEvent=NULL. Assign interruptConfig to channelConfig[0].interrupt and NULL to the others. (6) Configure channelConfig[0..2] with: timerCh = IfxGtm_Pwm_SubModule_Ch_0/1/2 (logical indices), requestFrequency = 20 kHz, center-aligned mode, complementary outputs enabled, and initial requestDuty values = {25.0f, 50.0f, 75.0f}. Do not use physical TOM channel numbers; rely on the driver to map outputs within the selected cluster. (7) Set mainConfig.cluster to the required TOM1 Cluster_1 (per requirements), enable synchronous start/update (syncStart = TRUE), and assign pointers to output[], dtmConfig[], channelConfig[] and their counts. (8) GTM enable guard: Check IfxGtm_isEnabled(&MODULE_GTM); if not enabled, call IfxGtm_enable(&MODULE_GTM), then dynamically read the module frequency via IfxGtm_Cmu_getModuleFrequency(&MODULE_GTM), set GCLK to that value via IfxGtm_Cmu_setGclkFrequency(&MODULE_GTM, freq), and enable clocks via IfxGtm_Cmu_enableClocks(&MODULE_GTM, IFXGTM_CMU_CLKEN_FXCLK | IFXGTM_CMU_CLKEN_CLK0). (9) Call IfxGtm_Pwm_init(&g_state.pwm, &g_state.channels[0], &mainConfig) to apply configuration. (10) Persist runtime state: store initial dutyCycles[3] = {25,50,75} and deadTimes[3] = {1us,1us,1us} in the module state alongside the IfxGtm_Pwm handle and a persistent IfxGtm_Pwm_Channel channels[3] array (the channels array must be persistent because the driver holds its pointer). (11) Configure the LED GPIO as push-pull output AFTER PWM init (P13.0 in hardware) using IfxPort_setPinModeOutput; initial state remains default (no explicit drive low here). The PWM driver will start channel outputs synchronously at init due to syncStart=TRUE.

Update the three phase duties in percent using a +10% step with wrap rule. Algorithm: read current dutyCycles[3] from the module state; for each index i in {0,1,2} add STEP=10.0f; if (duty[i] + STEP) >= 100.0f, set duty[i] = 0.0f before adding STEP, then add STEP to get the new duty; clamp at 100% exclusive so values remain in [0,100). Store updated dutyCycles back into state. Build a 3-element float32 array requestDuty[] from the updated duties in the same {U,V,W} order and call the unified driver API to apply the new duties immediately and synchronously across the configured channels.

Empty period callback function required by the unified PWM driver interrupt configuration. The function intentionally performs no work. It is invoked from the driver's internal ISR context on PWM period events when configured.
